# Media Gallery Selection — Kaggle Submission

**Plan owner:** [2026-05-18-submission-plan.md](./2026-05-18-submission-plan.md) Phase B4 (selection) → Phase G (final assembly)
**Source bundle:** `output/playwright/ui-evidence/2026-04-27T01-22-55-961Z/` (auto-regenerated by `npm run ui:evidence`; refresh before final submission to pick up any UI changes)

The Kaggle submission requires:

- **Cover image** (1 required) — the first visual a judge sees on the writeup card.
- **Additional gallery images** (3-5 recommended) — supporting visuals attached to the writeup.

Per the submission rules, all gallery items go under the writeup's "Media Gallery" attachment list.

---

## Cover image candidates (pick one)

| Candidate | Path | Why it works | Why it might not |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A. Differentiate panel mid-result** | `differentiate-desktop.png` | Shows the multimodal-adjacent magic — five readiness-aligned variants visible at once. Strongest "this is what the product does" single image. | Variant cards are dense; small thumbnails may not read clearly. |
| **B. Today panel** | `today-desktop.png` | Shows the bird's-eye operating layer — recommended next move, classroom roster snippet, coverage signals. Clean hierarchy. | Less visceral than seeing actual generated content. |
| **C. Tomorrow Plan with retrieval trace** | `tomorrow-plan-desktop.png` | Demonstrates the closed loop in a single screen — plan output + cited classroom-memory sources. The "pattern_informed: true" badge sells the architecture. | Requires a judge to read context to understand why retrieval matters. |
| **D. Tomorrow Plan dark mode** | `tomorrow-plan-dark-desktop.png` | Distinctive aesthetic; shows accessibility + design-system maturity. Stands out in a sea of light-mode submissions. | Dark mode in social-share previews can render unpredictably. |

**Recommendation:** **Candidate A (Differentiate panel)**. The hackathon judges will see hundreds of submissions; the cover image's job is to communicate "this is a real working product" in 200×200 px. The five-variant grid does that better than any other surface.

If A reads too dense in thumbnail testing, fall back to **C (Tomorrow Plan with retrieval trace)** — the second most product-distinctive shot.

---

## Supporting gallery images (4-5)

Recommended set, sequenced for narrative flow on the writeup card:

1. `today-desktop.png` — establish the operating-layer framing
2. `differentiate-desktop.png` — show the five-variant magic (or use this as cover and substitute another)
3. `tomorrow-plan-desktop.png` — show the closed loop in action
4. `family-message-desktop.png` — show the safety story (approval gate visible)
5. `shell-mobile.png` — show the responsive design / pilot-readiness

If only 3 spots are available, drop #4 (mobile) — safety story is in the video.

---

## Architecture diagram

The Kaggle writeup §2 includes a Mermaid closed-loop diagram (added to [docs/architecture.md](../architecture.md) "Closed feedback loop" section on 2026-04-26). Kaggle markdown supports Mermaid natively; render directly in the writeup body.

If the cover image needs an architecture diagram instead of a UI screenshot, render the Mermaid diagram to PNG via `mmdc` (Mermaid CLI) or via the GitHub viewer screenshot. Format: 1280×720 PNG, light background, single-line title at top.

---

## Capture refresh checklist (pre-submission)

Before final submission, regenerate the UI evidence bundle to catch any drift since 2026-04-27:

```bash
nvm use && npm run ui:evidence
```

Then update the `Source bundle` reference at the top of this doc.

---

## Asset locations once selected

After the final selection is made, **do not commit binaries to the repo** (the `output/playwright/` path is gitignored for a reason — these are large PNGs that would bloat the public repo). Instead:

1. Pick the final cover + supporting images.
2. Upload them directly to the Kaggle writeup's Media Gallery attachment field.
3. Note the Kaggle attachment IDs in [hackathon-submission-checklist.md](../hackathon-submission-checklist.md) "Existing media candidates" section once attached.
4. Tick the corresponding checkbox in the submission plan Phase G.

If a public landing-page-style cover with text overlay is desired, build it externally (Figma / Canva / Photoshop) using the chosen UI screenshot as the base layer; do not commit the layered source file to the repo either.
